item.title: Th1/Th17 cytokine signatures correlate with Crohn's disease activity in a 54‑week cohort of patients treated with adalimumab.

item.title: Human factors validation study of a disposable autoinjector (Bmab 1200 autoinjector) for subcutaneous delivery of Ustekinumab.
item.description: Drug delivery - Ustekinumab is approved for treating moderate to severe plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, Crohn's disease, and ulcerative colitis. The Bmab 1200 autoinjector is a simple, two-step device for s...

item.title: Non-cirrhotic hepatocellular carcinoma during Crohn's disease therapy with azathioprine : possible involvement of focal hepatocyte glycogenosis in hepatocarcinogenesis.
item.description: Clinical journal of gastroenterology - Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) rarely develops in patients with Crohn's disease (CD) without cirrhosis. Focal hepatic glycogenosis (FHG), characterized by localized hepatocellular glycogen accumula...

item.title: Comparison of Ustekinumab and Vedolizumab Effectiveness in Perianal Crohn's Disease: A Retrospective Propensity Matched Cohort Study.

item.title: Comment on "Real-world outcomes of ustekinumab, vedolizumab, and tumor necrosis factor inhibitors in very-early-onset inflammatory bowel disease": treatment comparison or treatment sequencing?
A decade of clinical data with vedolizumab: the past, present, and future.

item.title: Regression of vascular wall thickening with upadacitinib in a patient with clinically silent pediatric-onset takayasu arteritis and ulcerative colitis: case-based review.
